title: Error: capset: Operation not permitted: OCI permission denied
body:
/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
I am getting below error upon running podman on podman.

time="2022-03-01T03:31:42Z" level=debug msg="running conmon: /usr/bin/conmon" args="[--api-version 1 -c e80ffa4e910b190c42db598beeb0cf1fad48c97655c5c3bf278afe2ed8462e8a -u e80ffa4e910b190c42db598beeb0cf1fad48c97655c5c3bf278afe2ed8462e8a -r /usr/bin/crun -b /var/lib/containers/storage/vfs-containers/e80ffa4e910b190c42db598beeb0cf1fad48c97655c5c3bf278afe2ed8462e8a/userdata -p /run/containers/storage/vfs-containers/e80ffa4e910b190c42db598beeb0cf1fad48c97655c5c3bf278afe2ed8462e8a/userdata/pidfile -n jolly_feynman --exit-dir /run/libpod/exits --socket-dir-path /run/libpod/socket -l k8s-file:/var/lib/containers/storage/vfs-containers/e80ffa4e910b190c42db598beeb0cf1fad48c97655c5c3bf278afe2ed8462e8a/userdata/ctr.log --log-level debug --syslog --runtime-arg --cgroup-manager --runtime-arg disabled --conmon-pidfile /run/containers/storage/vfs-containers/e80ffa4e910b190c42db598beeb0cf1fad48c97655c5c3bf278afe2ed8462e8a/userdata/conmon.pid --exit-command /usr/bin/podman --exit-command-arg --root --exit-command-arg /var/lib/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --runroot --exit-command-arg /run/containers/storage --exit-command-arg --log-level --exit-command-arg debug --exit-command-arg --cgroup-manager --exit-command-arg cgroupfs --exit-command-arg --tmpdir --exit-command-arg /run/libpod --exit-command-arg --runtime --exit-command-arg crun --exit-command-arg --storage-driver --exit-command-arg vfs --exit-command-arg --events-backend --exit-command-arg file --exit-command-arg --syslog --exit-command-arg container --exit-command-arg cleanup --exit-command-arg --rm --exit-command-arg e80ffa4e910b190c42db598beeb0cf1fad48c97655c5c3bf278afe2ed8462e8a]"
time="2022-03-01T03:31:42Z" level=debug msg="Received: -1"
time="2022-03-01T03:31:42Z" level=debug msg="Cleaning up container e80ffa4e910b190c42db598beeb0cf1fad48c97655c5c3bf278afe2ed8462e8a"
time="2022-03-01T03:31:42Z" level=debug msg="Network is already cleaned up, skipping..."
time="2022-03-01T03:31:42Z" level=debug msg="unmounted container \"e80ffa4e910b190c42db598beeb0cf1fad48c97655c5c3bf278afe2ed8462e8a\""
time="2022-03-01T03:31:42Z" level=debug msg="Removing container e80ffa4e910b190c42db598beeb0cf1fad48c97655c5c3bf278afe2ed8462e8a"
time="2022-03-01T03:31:42Z" level=debug msg="Removing all exec sessions for container e80ffa4e910b190c42db598beeb0cf1fad48c97655c5c3bf278afe2ed8462e8a"
time="2022-03-01T03:31:42Z" level=debug msg="Cleaning up container e80ffa4e910b190c42db598beeb0cf1fad48c97655c5c3bf278afe2ed8462e8a"
time="2022-03-01T03:31:42Z" level=debug msg="Network is already cleaned up, skipping..."
time="2022-03-01T03:31:42Z" level=debug msg="Container e80ffa4e910b190c42db598beeb0cf1fad48c97655c5c3bf278afe2ed8462e8a storage is already unmounted, skipping..."
time="2022-03-01T03:31:42Z" level=debug msg="Container e80ffa4e910b190c42db598beeb0cf1fad48c97655c5c3bf278afe2ed8462e8a storage is already unmounted, skipping..."
time="2022-03-01T03:31:42Z" level=debug msg="ExitCode msg: \"capset: operation not permitted: oci permission denied\""
Error: capset: Operation not permitted: OCI permission denied
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

while running below command on jenkins
Debian11 image.


1. sudo podman run --rm --storage-driver=vfs --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs --log-level debug --privileged docker.io/multiarch/qemu-user-static --reset -p yes

**Describe the results you received:**

observed below error
time="2022-03-01T03:31:42Z" level=debug msg="ExitCode msg: \"capset: operation not permitted: oci permission denied\""
Error: capset: Operation not permitted: OCI permission denied


**Describe the results you expected:**
image should have pulled.

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
  Version: 3.0.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```
podman/stable,now 3.0.1+dfsg1-3+b2 amd64 [installed]
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide? (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/troubleshooting.md)**


Yes, latest version i get from apt is 3.0.1

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
AWS, Jenkins,.

comments.body:
@giuseppe 

grep ap /proc/self/status
VmSwap:	       0 kB
CapInh:	00000000a92425fb
CapPrm:	0000000000000000
CapEff:	0000000000000000
CapBnd:	00000000a92425fb
CapAmb:	0000000000000000

so that is the issue, you are running in an environment without capabilities but trying to use them.

How has Jenkins created this environment?  Does it work if you use `--cap-drop=all` for your container?

@giuseppe 
  
spec:
    containers:
    - name: container-image-builder
      image: docker.xxxxx.com/personal/aramsamy/debian11-podman:1.0.0
      tty: true
      command: [ "cat" ]
      imagePullPolicy: Always
      securityContext:
        capabilities:
          add: [ "SYS_ADMIN", "SYS_RESOURCE" ]
      volumeMounts:
        - name: container-storage
          mountPath: /var/lib/containers
        - name: workspace-volume
          mountPath: /home/jenkins/agent
      resources:
        limits: 
          github.com/fuse: 1

Error: capset: Operation not permitted: OCI permission denied

if you try to run a privileged container, then it means all the capabilities will be used.

Please drop `--privileged`.

Also, if you run the pod without `cap_sys_admin`, podman will try to create a user namespace automatically and gain capabilities there.  Please try creating the pod without `cap_sys_admin`, does it make any difference?

I am closing the issue because this is just a configuration problem, but please feel free to comment further
problem is, with same capabilities on Fedora image it works fine without any issues. But on debian11 its failing with above errors.

on Fedora image i see storage.conf but not on debian11.

Here is the list of packages i am installing on debian.

  apt-get -y install python3 && \
  apt-get -y install apt-transport-https ca-certificates curl gnupg2 software-properties-common vim python3-pip jq groff awscli \
  libgpgme-dev libassuan-dev libdevmapper-dev lsb-release pkgconf screen unzip skopeo qemu binfmt-support qemu-user-static fuse-overlayfs podman

yum -y install podman fuse-overlayfs --exclude container-selinux;
I think it could be a different set of default capabilities in `/etc/containers/containers.conf`
